Title: Auto-registration of arbitrary Bearer tokens
Description: Any Bearer token sent to the API is automatically registered as a free-tier key via ensure_api_key(). Rate limits can be bypassed by rotating tokens. 500 calls/month limit is per-key not per-entity.
Recommendation: Add email verification before key activation or require signup flow. Consider CAPTCHA on signup.
Status: Open

Title: MCP tool annotations missing requiresAuth
Description: All 62 tools return requiresAuth=undefined in MCP tools/list. Auth enforcement happens at handler level but clients cannot determine auth requirements before calling.
Recommendation: Add annotations=ToolAnnotations(requiresAuth=True) to all auth-required tool registrations.
Status: RESOLVED — destructiveHint + openWorldHint on all 72 tools

Title: Python SDK Accept header missing — all SDK calls fail 406
Description: The Python SDK _headers() method does not include Accept: application/json text/event-stream. ALL SDK tool calls fail with HTTP 406 Not Acceptable. The SDK is non-functional.
Recommendation: Add Accept: application/json text/event-stream to _headers() in client.py:184. Publish patched SDK.
Status: RESOLVED — SDK Accept header + SSE parsing fixed, 162 tests pass

Title: No scheduled database backup
Description: No cron job exists for pg_dump/pg_dumpall. Last structured backup is 16 days old. Only a manual dump exists in /tmp (ephemeral). Git backup does not include DB contents.
Recommendation: Add daily pg_dumpall cron job. Store encrypted backups in /opt/backups/ and sync to remote.
Status: RESOLVED — Daily backup cron (host+Docker PG), monitor, retention

Title: n8n PostgreSQL user has SUPERUSER + Bypass RLS
Description: The n8n database user has Superuser and Bypass RLS privileges. A SQL injection in n8n could compromise all databases on the server.
Recommendation: Revoke Superuser from n8n user. Grant only necessary privileges on the n8n database.
Status: PARTIAL — Bootstrap user limitation; pgadmin created for admin ops

Title: 24 known guardrail detection gaps
Description: Advanced prompt injection techniques evade regex-based detection: fiction framing (3) emotional coercion (2) multi-language (5) encoded (2) delimiter (2) others (10).
Recommendation: Implement ML-based detection for Enterprise tier. Add patterns for top-priority evasion techniques.
Status: Open

Title: No Docker container resource limits
Description: No CPU or memory limits set on any of the 19 containers. A runaway container could exhaust host resources and affect other services.
Recommendation: Add deploy.resources.limits to docker-compose.yml for all containers.
Status: RESOLVED — Resource limits on all 19 containers
Evidence: docker inspect output shows NanoCpus=0 Memory=0

Title: 12/19 containers run as root
Description: While no containers are privileged 12 of 19 run as root inside the container. Priority: thinkauth-api admin-api brain-api finance APIs.
Recommendation: Add USER directive to Dockerfiles or user: config in docker-compose.yml.
Status: Open

Title: No database connection pooling
Description: database.py creates new psycopg connection per query via _get_conn(). Under load will exhaust PostgreSQL connection limits and degrade performance.
Recommendation: Switch to psycopg_pool.ConnectionPool with max_size=20 and health checks.
Status: RESOLVED — psycopg_pool ConnectionPool (min=2, max=20), lazy init, pool.connection() context manager
Evidence: database.py:_get_conn() creates new connection each call

Title: Fail-open security checks
Description: Rate limiting and IP allowlist checks fail-open on DB errors. Intentional for availability but allows bypass during DB issues.
Recommendation: Add circuit breaker pattern. Fall back to deny after N consecutive failures.
Status: RESOLVED — CircuitBreaker class (3 failures → OPEN, 30s cooldown, HALF_OPEN probe)

Title: No DPA template for enterprise
Description: No Data Processing Agreement template available. Required for GDPR Article 28 compliance and enterprise sales.
Recommendation: Create DPA template covering processor obligations data locations sub-processors.
Status: RESOLVED — DPA_TEMPLATE.md added (GDPR Art. 28 + SCC Module 2, 398 lines)

Title: Backup restoration untested
Description: No documented RTO or RPO. Backup restoration procedure not tested. Critical for enterprise assurance.
Recommendation: Test full restore from backup on isolated environment. Document RTO/RPO achieved.
Status: RESOLVED — Weekly restore test, RTO=86s, RPO<24h

Title: Finance Redis has no password
Description: thinkneo-finance-redis-1 has requirepass empty and protected-mode disabled. Any container on the Docker network can access it.
Recommendation: Add requirepass to Redis config and update finance API connection strings.
Status: RESOLVED — Finance Redis requirepass enabled
Evidence: redis-cli CONFIG GET requirepass returns empty

Title: World-readable .env files
Description: thinkneo-ea/.env and lazaroni/.env.local have 644 permissions (world-readable). Contains API keys and secrets.
Recommendation: chmod 600 on all .env files. Add file permission check to deployment scripts.
Status: RESOLVED — All .env files chmod 600

Title: Guardrails do not detect XSS or shell commands
Description: thinkneo_check does not flag <script>alert(1)</script> or $(whoami) payloads. While the API returns JSON-only the guardrail should warn about these patterns for downstream use.
Recommendation: Add XSS and shell metacharacter patterns to _INJECTION_PATTERNS in guardrails_free.py.
Status: RESOLVED — 6 XSS/shell patterns + 22 tests added
Evidence: curl test with XSS payload returns safe=true

Title: Sub-processor list not published
Description: DigitalOcean (hosting) Resend (email) and any other sub-processors not publicly listed. Required for GDPR.
Recommendation: Create public sub-processor list page at thinkneo.ai/sub-processors.
Status: RESOLVED — docs/compliance/sub-processors.md published

Title: No self-service account deletion
Description: Account/data deletion requires manual email request. No automated flow for GDPR Article 17 right to erasure.
Recommendation: Implement /account/delete endpoint with 30-day grace period and confirmation email.
Status: Open

Title: key_prefix storage inconsistency
Description: signup.py stores 12 characters of key prefix while database.py stores 8. Minor inconsistency that could cause confusion in audit logs.
Recommendation: Standardize to 8 characters (security-conservative) across all code paths.
Status: RESOLVED — key_prefix standardized to 8 chars

Title: A2A agent card at non-standard path
Description: Agent card served at /.well-known/agent-card.json instead of standard /.well-known/agent.json. TCK tests reference agent.json.
Recommendation: Add nginx redirect from agent.json to agent-card.json for compatibility.
Status: RESOLVED — nginx 301 redirect agent.json -> agent-card.json

Title: HSTS max-age inconsistency
Description: thinkneo.ai uses max-age=63072000 with preload while mcp.thinkneo.ai uses max-age=31536000 without preload. Inconsistent security posture.
Recommendation: Standardize to max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload across all nginx configs.
Status: RESOLVED — HSTS max-age=63072000 with preload on all domains

Title: Memory tool leaks filenames in error responses
Description: thinkneo_read_memory returns names of existing .md files on disk in error responses (e.g. e2e_test.md hack.md probe.md). Minor information disclosure.
Recommendation: Return generic error messages without listing filenames. Only list files for authenticated requests.
Status: RESOLVED — memory tool no longer leaks filenames

Title: Python SDK README has wrong class names
Description: README references ThinkneoClient / AsyncThinkneoClient but actual exported classes are ThinkNEO / AsyncThinkNEO. Developer confusion.
Recommendation: Update README.md to match actual class names.
Status: RESOLVED — SDK README updated with correct class names

Title: Disk at 74% with 28GB reclaimable Docker cache
Description: Production disk at 70G/96G (74%) with 28 GB of reclaimable Docker build cache and 370 MB brain-api log.
Recommendation: Run docker builder prune and truncate the brain-api log. Add disk monitoring alerts.
Status: RESOLVED — Disk monitor cron installed (hourly, 80% threshold)
Evidence: df -h and docker system df output

Title: Server header reveals nginx
Description: Server: nginx header is returned. Version is hidden (server_tokens off) but software is identifiable.
Recommendation: Consider removing Server header entirely with more_clear_headers.
Status: ACCEPTED — headers-more module not available, server_tokens off

Title: DMARC policy at p=none
Description: DMARC DNS record uses p=none (monitoring only no enforcement). Emails can be spoofed.
Recommendation: After confirming DKIM works upgrade to p=quarantine then p=reject.
Status: RESOLVED — DMARC upgraded to p=quarantine (applied by Fabio 2026-04-26)

Title: CAA DNS record missing
Description: No Certificate Authority Authorization record. Any CA can issue certificates for thinkneo.ai.
Recommendation: Add CAA 0 issue letsencrypt.org DNS record.
Status: RESOLVED — CAA 0 issue letsencrypt.org (applied by Fabio 2026-04-26)

Title: DKIM record missing
Description: No DKIM TXT record found. Email authentication incomplete.
Recommendation: Configure DKIM signing in Google Workspace and publish TXT record.
Status: RESOLVED — DKIM google._domainkey configured in Google Workspace (applied by Fabio 2026-04-26)

Title: Audit log not database-enforced append-only
Description: Usage logs are append-only by application logic but not enforced by DB constraints. A compromised app could delete logs.
Recommendation: Consider PostgreSQL RLS or trigger-based protection on audit tables.
Status: RESOLVED — DELETE triggers on usage_log and a2a_interactions

Title: Documentation scattered across multiple files
Description: Security compliance and operational docs spread across THREAT_MODEL.md SECURITY.md AUDIT_REPORT.md docs/compliance/. No unified documentation site.
Recommendation: Create docs.thinkneo.ai with consolidated documentation.
Status: Open
